ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Da keŋ a yaalɩma nyinti a lɔbɩrɩma tɩŋgbaŋka gie me, jigidieke nyɩnyʋgɩka aŋaŋ tʋgɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan chʋʋsɩha; ta gaarɩŋ dɩ baaŋ nan bɩagɩ awɩ a juu a gaarɩ.
translated: Because you have come to ask me for help to protect your house and to build the new house; until the new house is completed and you return.

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Die wɩa die ba gbaŋ gbaŋ die ba juu haarɩŋ a gara jigidieke vuoŋ die dɩ wone.
translated: The one who laughs and laughs, unaware of the listening ear that brings deceitful words, remains there.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Naa die yiye mɩŋ a yi Ŋmɩŋ naazʋa Jerimaya die dɩ balla wudiekemba wo dɩ yi wusie. Die ʋ baarɩ,
translated: Our brave Jerimaya, who came from Ŋmɩŋ, took the ball and ran to save the situation. The team celebrated.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Ama n balɩ nɩ, halɩ naaŋ Solomoni aŋaŋ ʋ nyiŋyeeki vɩɩna mana die ka yeegi jayeekiŋ ka vɩɩna a tʋgɩ tɩɩwutehe gie.
translated: Today, the people of Solomon gathered to discuss the matter and decided to plan and organize, appointing a representative.

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Womi mɩraha dɩdagɩrɩba bataŋ aŋaŋ Farasisi bataŋ die dɩ balɩ Yisa dɩ, “Dɩdagɩrʋ tɩ yaala fʋ yiwo mamachi wɩɩŋ aŋ tɩ ye.”
translated: When the ruler of the Pharisees died, Jesus said, "The ruler has gone to his place and will not return again."

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.

translated: Hello, Juu Vuosi davusikir daraang, Yisa angang and the kvaandilsiriba have come to ask for your help. The matter is that the kvaandilsiriba went to gobe zaapakit because of the journey's reason.

translated: "Trying to assemble my belongings and take them, but the journey is long!"

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: Ʋ dagɩkʋ sʋŋ die ʋ balɩ a yɩ ba dɩ: “Nɩ wʋmma, daa wʋnyɩ die nyɩnna a ga dɩ ʋ bʋrɩ ʋ kʋaŋ ma.
translated: The day the sun died, the woman said: "My mother, give me the children so I can take them to the forest and the mountain."

ref: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
text: Ka suuline wo zaasɩyigiribe dɩ datɩ ka nyɩŋ gaaŋ ma, a sʋʋŋ kalɩ a lugi zaasɩvɩɩnaha a yi kparɩŋ ma ama ta nagɩ zaasɩbɩatɩtɩ a vigi taaŋ wa.
translated: When the speaker spoke, the people gathered together and listened, and they understood collectively, but when there was a problem, they discussed and found a solution.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
text: Saŋka mi manɩŋ naaŋ wa nan balɩ yɩ vuodiekemba dɩ benne n nuudiigiri chaakʋ dɩ, ‘Nɩnɩŋ vuodiekemba n Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ dɩ yine alibarika yɩ, nɩ keŋ a juu naarɩ dieke ta Ŋmɩŋ die dɩ naanna a gbarɩ nɩ a nyɩŋ dʋnɩa piiliŋ me,
translated: Saŋka, my friend, I have come to know that you are going on a journey to the distant place called Nuudiigiri, and I am going with Chʋa Ŋmɩŋ to find the elusive alibarika, and I want to take the sacred object from Ŋmɩŋ's house so that we can use it to unlock the sealed piiliŋ in the dʋnɩa.
